preview_text:
The model favors Iran at 65%, nearly twice the probability it assigns to a New Zealand victory at 10%. The statistical forecast sees a substantial gap between the two sides, though the draw probability of 25% suggests the match retains meaningful uncertainty.

The win-loss spread indicates Iran is the clear favorite, but a quarter chance of a stalemate reflects competitive balance in the middle ground. A New Zealand upset remains possible at one in ten, while draws neither rare nor dominant in the model's view.

The model and market diverge notably here. The sportsbook consensus gives Iran only 51%, some 14 percentage points below the model estimate, while assigning New Zealand 22%—more than double the model's 10%. Polymarket aligns closely with sportsbooks. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap warrants attention from users assessing which framework better reflects underlying match dynamics.
